task: Replace the Ana friend and ally strategy-document pages with a decision-ready HR team experience, add a Cecilia proposal room, and align the Ana download and portal surfaces with the maintained HR Operations workflow.
audience: Ana Cecilia Cancino, her four-person HR team, and Frank as the proposed build and operations partner.
first_read: Ana already has the method; the system makes it teachable without giving AI the decisions.

references.label: Ana HR Operations
references.reason: Defines the real procedure sequence, private-record contract, Google Docs route, and human approval points.

references.label: FrankX brand pack
references.reason: Defines technical luxury, restrained signal color, direct founder language, and evidence over spectacle.

references.label: Ana and Frank meeting notes
references.reason: Provides the actual document and recruiting bottlenecks without exposing client or candidate data.

keep: Warm bronze and forest bridge palette
Private review state until Ana approves indexing
Real links to Ana's site, LinkedIn, GitHub kit, and FrankX portal
Human approval for pricing, hiring, invoices, and sends
avoid: Named fantasy agents and AI-swarm diagrams
Unverified biography, neuroscience, psychology, and income claims
Fake dashboards, decorative 3D, orbit diagrams, and generated portrait substitutes
direction.signature: A keyboard-accessible stage explorer for Ana's exact HR workflow, paired with a consented proposal response.
system: Status vocabulary that separates available, pilot, proposal, and decision states
Native responsive composition and a complete reduced-motion state
Existing FrankX intake route for notification and durable response handling

asset_quality.why_premium: The primary visual is an exact and useful representation of Ana's verified operating process, not decorative imagery.

checks.name: asset-tier
checks.evidence: Tier C is the correct medium for process stages, responsibilities, status, and accessible interaction.

checks.name: source-and-claims-audit
checks.evidence: Drive notes, live deployment state, current kit repository, domain responses, and public LinkedIn results were reviewed; unverified claims were excluded.

checks.name: implementation
checks.evidence: Five Ana surfaces, the exact workflow explorer, proposal response form, current release endpoint, shared data, and a legacy-page removal are implemented.

checks.name: scoped-code-validation
checks.evidence: Seven Ana contract tests, scoped ESLint, scoped TypeScript, and git diff whitespace checks pass.

checks.name: ana-copy-audit
checks.evidence: Ana-facing files contain none of the obsolete Freedom Engine, agent-pack, ACOS, passive-income, public-safe, synthetic, or autonomous-recruiter language.

checks.name: local-build
checks.evidence: PP build preflight returned HOLD: 5.9 GB free versus the required 8 GB reserve, with 15 task runtimes versus a budget of 12.

checks.name: export-inspection
checks.evidence: Desktop and mobile preview inspection waits for a Vercel preview and browser-workload admission.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: Responsive, keyboard, reduced-motion, and contrast inspection is not claimed until actual preview exports are inspected.

score.notes: Information architecture, verified copy, asset choice, responsive source, accessibility contract, and code checks are strong. Actual desktop/mobile export inspection remains required before ship status.

known_risks: No approved Ana portrait is available, so the hero uses verified workflow proof.
The current Ana site contains biography language that needs Ana verification in a separate repository session.
Cecilia identity, economics, payment tooling, DNS, and launch remain human-gated.
Machine preflight held full build and browser work during this pass.
